summary: Lead accepts M4 grain supply reservations and consumption forecast after systems_architect R2 ACCEPT and lead integration gates. The task adds explicit grain reserve/consume/release commands and events, M2 stock deltas, conservation-aware loss/shortage accounting, stable reservation identity validation, protocol parsing, and tests while staying out of route capacity, march, UI, battle/siege, save-format milestone, and full economy scope.

tests_run.result: Passed architecture boundaries, determinism, root Vitest 28 files / 187 tests, and web tests 1 file / 3 tests.
Passed format, lint, workspace typecheck, and architecture boundary checks.

tests_run.command: git diff scope scan with rg
tests_run.result: No forbidden nondeterministic APIs, UI/platform imports, save-format scope, battle/siege/march/market/naval terms, or explicit any usage in relevant diff.

decisions: Accepted after systems_architect verified all R2 blockers were fixed.
No additional QA reviewer is required by this task file; reviewer_role is systems_architect for this R3 simulation/protocol task.
Package script change only adds the new grain supply test file; no dependency changes.
risks: Save-format milestone work remains out of scope and must be addressed by a later M4 save/runtime task before persistence claims include grain supply reservations.
Future route capacity/logistics tasks must not treat the bounded reserve event as a complete logistics audit stream; per-source authority remains in state rows and deltas.
requested_action: Commit accepted implementation, create PR, require CI, merge to main, sync, close task only after integration.
